Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

As you can see from the number of tests that need to be changed, changing the llapi function will also cause problems for our-of-tree applications that are linking to liblustreapi. 

The old llapi functions should continue to take an "int" as the argument, but store it into a "long" before calling the ioctl.

If there really a need for a 64-bit group lock ID?  If yes, then new llapi_ functions should be created (eg. llapi_group_lock64()) and use a __u64 for the argument.

Patch Set 1:

yeah, I am also concerned about the group lock API compatibility caused by the int to long change. Andreas, do you have any idea how widely/common group lock has been used?

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> As you can see from the number of tests that need to be changed,
 > changing the llapi function will also cause problems for
 > our-of-tree applications that are linking to liblustreapi.
 > 
 > The old llapi functions should continue to take an "int" as the
 > argument, but store it into a "long" before calling the ioctl.
 > 
 > If there really a need for a 64-bit group lock ID?  If yes, then
 > new llapi_ functions should be created (eg. llapi_group_lock64())
 > and use a __u64 for the argument.

originally i think it ok to change int to unsigned long.

If users really pass in a negative group id, it was wrong for old usage, As negative value is unexpected.

And if existed application passed in positive value, new unsigned long
could convert from int without problem?

Patch Set 1:

> As you can see from the number of tests that need to be changed,
 > changing the llapi function will also cause problems for
 > our-of-tree applications that are linking to liblustreapi.
 > 
 > The old llapi functions should continue to take an "int" as the
 > argument, but store it into a "long" before calling the ioctl.
 > 
 > If there really a need for a 64-bit group lock ID?  

Limit group id (2^31-1) is fine for snapshot usage of course too.

If yes, then
 > new llapi_ functions should be created (eg. llapi_group_lock64())
 > and use a __u64 for the argument.

Patch Set 1:

The promotion from int to __u64 at compile time is not the problem.  The main issue is that since liblustreapi is a shared library, this would cause an ABI change. That may cause either a runtime failure for applications using this function (eg. copytools or applications that use group lock), or worse - unpredictable results based on whatever garbage is on the stack above the "int" because the kernel is trying to access this as a "__u64".

It is better/safer to keep the current API/ABI for the old code, and add an llapi_group_lock64() function that does it properly, and use the new interface in the tools if needed.  At some point in the future (eg. 3 years) deprecate this interface and new compiles should be redirected to use the 64-bit interface.

Patch Set 1:

 > failure for applications using this function (eg. copytools or
 > applications that use group lock), or worse - unpredictable results
 > based on whatever garbage is on the stack above the "int" because
 > the kernel is trying to access this as a "__u64".
 > 
 > It is better/safer to keep the current API/ABI for the old code,

 > use the new interface in the tools if needed.  At some point in the
 > future (eg. 3 years) deprecate this interface and new compiles
 > should be redirected to use the 64-bit interface.

Make sense, thanks for detailed information.

commit.subject: LU-14612 utils: Add functions llapi_group_lock64/unlock64()
commit.message:
LU-14612 utils: Add functions llapi_group_lock64/unlock64()

Add new functions llapi_group_lock64/unlock64() for 64-bit usage.
